expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: donut-chart data label coverage distinguishes label visibility, density, and overlap-sensitive outcomes
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states the required focus and enumerates the three distinctions: "Focus (must be explicitly covered): donut-chart data label coverage that distinguishes **label visibility**, **label density**, and **overlap-sensitive outcomes**." It also reiterates in Section 1: "explicitly include donut-chart data label scenarios that separate: 1. Label visibility ... 2. Label density ... 3. Overlap-sensitive outcomes..."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The document is framed as a phase4a checkpoint review throughout: title "QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 4a Checkpoint Review" and "Primary phase under test: phase4a". It also defines phase4a expectations in Section 1 ("Phase 4a ... should validate that the QA plan’s test coverage is complete and discriminating") and includes a "Phase 4a Alignment Check" section, even though it concludes alignment cannot be confirmed due to missing artifacts.
